summary: Blood glucose estimation is not required in Rh incompatility because its primary pathophysiology revolves around immune-mediated hemolysis causing hyperbilirubinemia, not metabolic disturbances involving glucose regulation. Therefore, evaluating blood sugar does not contribute meaningfully to diagnosis or management of Rh incompatibility.
